title_en: Salah in Kitab al-Umm: The Founder's Prayer Teachings
title_ar: الصلاة في كتاب الأم: تعاليم الإمام المؤسس في الصلاة
content_en:
The prayer chapter of Kitab al-Umm is among the most important sources for understanding the Shafi'i school's prayer law in its original formulation — before the later developments of ar-Rafi'i, an-Nawawi, and their commentators. Ash-Shafi'i's positions, set out in his own words with his own arguments, provide the foundation on which the entire subsequent Shafi'i prayer tradition was built.

Ash-Shafi'i opens the prayer chapter by establishing the absolute centrality of prayer in Islam: it is the first obligation after the testimony of faith, it is repeated five times daily, and its abandonment — according to the authentic hadiths — brings the person to the edge of disbelief. He quotes the hadith 'The distinguishing line between a person and disbelief and polytheism is the abandonment of prayer' (Muslim) and explains why the Shafi'i school, following the hadith's plain meaning, treats deliberate abandonment of prayer as a grave matter requiring the most serious legal response.

On the conditions of prayer, ash-Shafi'i's treatment of facing the qiblah in Kitab al-Umm is historically significant: he established the Shafi'i position that those who can see the Ka'bah must face it exactly, while those too distant must face the general direction of Makkah (jihat al-Ka'bah) based on their best effort. This distinction between 'ayn al-Ka'bah (the exact building) and jihat al-Ka'bah (the direction of Makkah) became the standard Shafi'i position.

Ash-Shafi'i's discussion of reciting al-Fatiha in every rak'ah is one of the positions for which he is most known and which most clearly distinguishes the Shafi'i school. He quotes the hadith 'There is no prayer for one who does not recite the Opening of the Book' (Bukhari, Muslim) and argues that its generality ('there is no prayer') covers every rak'ah, not just the first. He addresses the Hanafi objection that reciting al-Fatiha in the third and fourth rak'ahs of quiet prayers is a burden — ash-Shafi'i responds that following authentic hadith is the obligation of every Muslim regardless of perceived difficulty.

On the question of reciting the basmala aloud, ash-Shafi'i argues in Kitab al-Umm for the audible basmala (jahr bi-l-basmala) in the loud prayers — based on hadiths showing the Prophet saying the basmala as part of al-Fatiha and the companions' practice of saying it aloud. This position is one where the Shafi'i school in Egypt (following ash-Shafi'i's Egyptian-period teaching) differs from the Iraqi period opinion, and ash-Shafi'i's Egyptian-period argument for the audible basmala is found in Kitab al-Umm.

The qunoot supplication in Fajr prayer is established in Kitab al-Umm as a confirmed Prophetic sunnah. Ash-Shafi'i presents the hadiths showing the Prophet consistently performing qunoot in the Fajr prayer and explains why the Shafi'i school — unlike the Hanafi school, which holds qunoot was abrogated or restricted to cases of calamity — maintains it as an ongoing sunnah of the prayer.

Ash-Shafi'i's engagement with the Hanafi school on prayer matters throughout Kitab al-Umm is among the most intellectually stimulating aspects of the work. His critiques are direct, his arguments textual, and his conclusions carefully reasoned. For any student who wants to understand why the Shafi'i and Hanafi schools differ on prayer — and what the evidence basis for each position is — Kitab al-Umm provides indispensable primary-source insight.
title_id: Shalat dalam Kitab al-Umm: Ajaran Shalat Pendiri
content_id:
Bab shalat dalam Kitab al-Umm adalah di antara sumber-sumber terpenting untuk memahami hukum shalat madzhab Syafi'i dalam rumusan aslinya — sebelum perkembangan-perkembangan selanjutnya dari ar-Rafi'i, an-Nawawi, dan teks-teks kanonik belakangan. Membaca asy-Syafi'i tentang shalat memberikan akses ke proses pengambilan keputusan hukum: mengapa madzhab Syafi'i mewajibkan membaca Al-Fatihah dalam setiap rakaat shalat, mengapa ia membutuhkan niat yang tepat pada waktu takbiratul ihram, dan mengapa ia menetapkan persyaratan-persyaratan tertentu untuk tasyahud.

Keterlibatan asy-Syafi'i dengan tradisi Maliki dan Hanafi tentang shalat sangat mendidik dalam Kitab al-Umm. Beliau mengetahui posisi-posisi Maliki dan Hanafi dari studi langsung, dan ketika beliau berbeda dari keduanya, beliau mengartikulasikan alasan-alasannya. Ini menjadikan Kitab al-Umm sumber primer yang tak ternilai untuk sejarah perselisihan antar-madzhab tentang hukum shalat, terutama untuk memahami apa yang asy-Syafi'i sendiri anggap sebagai penalaran paling kuat untuk posisi-posisi Syafi'i.

Shalat berjamaah, shalat Jumat, dan shalat-shalat khusus menerima perlakuan rinci dalam Kitab al-Umm yang mencerminkan perhatian asy-Syafi'i baik terhadap dimensi hukum shalat dan dimensi spiritualnya. Diskusinya tentang imam dan makmum, tentang kondisi-kondisi yang membuat mengikuti imam tertentu tidak diperbolehkan, dan tentang bagaimana menangani berbagai keadaan darurat yang muncul selama shalat mencerminkan perhatian praktis seorang hukum yang terlibat dengan pertanyaan-pertanyaan nyata dari kehidupan komunal Muslim.
content_ar:
يتناول هذا الفصل أحكام الصلاة في كتاب الأم للإمام الشافعي. ويُفصّل في أركان الصلاة وشروطها وسننها، مع الاستناد الدقيق إلى أحاديث النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم في هيئة الصلاة.

ومن أبرز ما يتميز به المذهب الشافعي في الصلاة: رفع اليدين عند الركوع وعند الرفع منه، والجهر بالبسملة، وقراءة الفاتحة على المأموم، وهيئة الجلوس في التشهد. ويُوثّق الشافعي هذه المسائل بأحاديث صحيحة.

ويتناول الكتاب أيضاً أحكام صلاة الجماعة والجمعة والخوف والسفر والعيدين والاستسقاء والكسوف، مع بيان موقف الشافعي من مخالفيه من أصحاب المذاهب الأخرى.
